name: Duke Jordan
abstract: Irving Sidney "Duke" Jordan (April 1, 1922 – August 8, 2006) was an American jazz pianist.

concerts.label: Jazz Concert featuring Duke Ellington, Billie Holiday, Charlie Parker and Others

works.t: Strike up the Band: Strike up the Band
There Will Never Be Another You

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Jazz Concert featuring Duke Ellington, Billie Holiday, Charlie Parker and Others

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Gibbs
about_quotes.evidence: I think it was my first big record date with Allen Eager... we were recording and Duke... I walked over to Duke... I whispered in his ear... Duke wouldn’t tell me the chord changes
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Gibbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 5, 2021 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Percy Heath
about_quotes.evidence: Ben Webster, Duke Jordan and a drummer, Arthur Edgehill, we were playing up there behind Phil Black
about_quotes.doc_title: Percy Heath — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Bert
about_quotes.evidence: I had Duke Jordan on piano all the time... I worked Birdland and The Bohemia
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Bert interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie McLean
about_quotes.evidence: I saw that he was at the Three Deuces with his own band, with Miles, Duke Jordan, Max Roach, Tommy Potter.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie McLean — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Burrell
about_quotes.evidence: I found myself sitting on the stage with Bird, Miles [laughs], Duke Jordan, and Max, unbelievable.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Burrell — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Wilbur Ware
about_quotes.evidence: I used with Duke Jordan on piano and I forget who was on drums, to have the gigs pretty regular
about_quotes.doc_title: 68088_Wilbur B. Ware, Sr oral history interview
